Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Walter Isard papers, 1938-1986.
Collection Number: 3959

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Walter Isard papers, 1938-1986.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
3959
Abstract:
Faculty and professional papers of Walter Isard (1919-2010), Economist and Peace Scientist. Includes records of professional organizations which he founded, including the Regional Science Association (RSA), Peace Research Society International and Peace Science Society International (PSSI).
Creator:
Isard, Walter.
Quanitities:
113.4 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Cornell professor of Economics (Regional Science and Peace Science) from 1979-2010. Also taught at Harvard, MIT and the University of Pennsylvania.
Founded the Regional Science Association (RSA) and Peace Science Society International (PSSI) and was the director and program chair for Economists Allied for Arms Reduction (ECAAR).
Graduated from Temple University (A.B., 1939) and Harvard (M.A., 1941; PhD, 1943)
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Records related to Walter Isard's career.
Material from professional organizations, especially the Regional Science Association and Peace Science Society International. Includes correspondence, administrative records, reports, and conference programs and papers, and publications such as the Journal of the RSA, Papers, and the Journal of Peace Science.
Records from his role as faculty at Cornell, Harvard, MIT and the University of Pennsylvania, including course materials and students' theses. Includes articles assembled for research, his notes, and drafts of his books and papers.
Includes alphabetized correspondence.
Also, a CD containing photos of Isard.
Commonly used acronyms/abbreviations include the following:
  1. AEA: American Economic Association
  2. ASSA: Allied Social Science Associations
  3. ECAAR: Economists Allied for Arms Reduction (formerly Economists Against the Arms Race)
  4. PEPS: Peace Economics and Peace Science (journal)
  5. PRSI: Peace Research Society International
  6. PSSI: Peace Science Society International
  7. RSA: Regional Science Association
  8. RSRI: Regional Science Research Institute
  9. WAAS: World Academy of Arts and Sciences
  10. WU: World University
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Walter Isard papers, #3959. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

American Economic Association
Allied Social Science Associations
Subjects:
Peace science.
Regional science.
Regional planning.
City planning.
Geography -- Research.
Physical geography.
Form and Genre Terms:
Resumes.
